title: The way I drink my morning coffee
summary: Describe your small daily ritual of drinking coffee in the morning.
topic: The way I drink my morning coffee
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about how you drink your coffee in the morning. Say when, where, and how you make it. Use present simple and adverbs of frequency.
sampleText: Every morning, I wake up at six. The kitchen in my small apartment in Buenos Aires is quiet at that time. I always make a cortado, which is a coffee with a little hot milk. First, I heat water in the kettle. Then I put two spoons of ground coffee in a small pot. I add the water and I stir slowly. I love the smell of the coffee. It is dark and warm. I pour the coffee into my favorite blue mug. The mug is old, but I never throw it away. I add a little milk, but I never add sugar because I like the strong taste. I take my mug to the balcony. I sit on a wooden chair and I sip the coffee slowly. I look at the street below. The buses pass, and the dogs walk with their owners. After the first cup, I feel ready for the day. This small ritual makes me happy.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for daily habits
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn cho thói quen hằng ngày: 'I wake up', 'I make', 'I add'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for daily habits, as in 'Every morning, I wake up at six'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'always', 'never' đặt trước động từ chính: 'I always make a cortado', 'I never add sugar'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'always' and 'never' go before the main verb, as in 'I always make a cortado' and 'I never add sugar'.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi sáng, tôi thức dậy lúc sáu giờ. Bếp trong căn hộ nhỏ của tôi ở Buenos Aires yên tĩnh vào giờ đó. Tôi luôn pha một ly cortado, là cà phê với một ít sữa nóng. Đầu tiên, tôi đun nước trong ấm. Sau đó tôi cho hai thìa cà phê xay vào một nồi nhỏ. Tôi đổ nước vào và khuấy chậm. Tôi yêu mùi cà phê. Nó đậm và ấm. Tôi rót cà phê vào chiếc cốc màu xanh yêu thích. Cốc cũ rồi nhưng tôi không bao giờ vứt đi. Tôi thêm một chút sữa, nhưng không bao giờ cho đường vì tôi thích vị đậm. Tôi mang cốc ra ban công. Tôi ngồi trên ghế gỗ và nhấp cà phê từ từ. Tôi nhìn xuống phố. Xe buýt chạy qua, và những chú chó đi dạo cùng chủ. Sau ly đầu tiên, tôi thấy mình sẵn sàng cho một ngày mới. Nghi thức nhỏ này khiến tôi hạnh phúc.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes a daily habit?
options: I am drinking coffee every morning.
Every morning, I drink coffee.
I drank coffee yesterday morning.
Coffee every morning I drinking.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple is the natural tense for everyday habits.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the present tense with a time phrase.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence places the adverb of frequency correctly?
options: I drink always my coffee black.
Always I drink my coffee black.
I always drink my coffee black.
I drink my coffee always black.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'always' goes before the main verb in the present simple.
mcQuestions.hint: The adverb sits between subject and main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option uses the correct preposition?
options: I sit in a chair on the balcony.
I sit on a chair in the balcony.
I sit at a chair on the balcony.
I sit in a chair in the balcony.
mcQuestions.explanation: We sit 'in' a chair (when it has arms) and 'on' a balcony.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about the surface and the enclosed space.

options: The coffee is having a strong taste.
The coffee has a strong taste.
The coffee is strong taste.
The coffee strongs the taste.
mcQuestions.explanation: Use 'has' for description; 'have' is not used in the continuous here.
mcQuestions.hint: Stative verbs use the simple form.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best fits the topic 'morning coffee ritual'?
options: I run five kilometres before breakfast.
I always make coffee at six and drink it slowly.
I check my phone all night.
I cook a big lunch for my family.
mcQuestions.explanation: It names time, drink, and pace — all parts of a coffee ritual.
mcQuestions.hint: Pick the option that mentions coffee and a calm pace.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which verb fits: 'I ___ the coffee into my mug.'
options: drop
pour
throw
kick
mcQuestions.explanation: 'pour' is the natural verb for liquids into a cup.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about how you move a liquid.

options: I drink coffee because it is strong but I do not add sugar.
I drink coffee but because strong sugar not.
Strong coffee I no sugar so.
Because I drink coffee sugar I add.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' gives a reason and 'but' shows contrast — both used clearly.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for clear linking with 'because' and 'but'.

options: She add milk to her coffee.
She adds milk to her coffee.
She adding milk to her coffee.
She added milk yesterday morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular adds -s in the present simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence describes the smell best?
options: The coffee smells dark and warm.
The coffee smell dark and warm.
The coffee is smell dark warm.
Dark warm smells the coffee.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option fits a coffee ritual best?
options: I drink it fast in the car.
I sip it slowly and watch the street.
I drink it while running.
I drink it before sleeping.
mcQuestions.explanation: Sipping slowly while watching the street fits a calm morning ritual.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small morning rituals like making coffee help some people feel ready for the day?
